SWATH OF DAMAGE EXTENDED FROM JUST NORTHEAST OF DEANVILLE TO JUST SOUTHEAST OF CALDWELL. DAMAGE APPEARED TORNADIC ALONG SOUTHERN PORTION OF THAT AREA WITH NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. ONE HOUSE WAS PUSHED OFF A CINDER BLOCK BASE THEN DRAGGED ALONG A CURVED PATH FOR ABOUT 100 FEET. A FEED TRAILER WAS LIFTED OVER A FENCE THEN ROLLED ABOUT 150 FEET. SEVERAL SHEDS AND BARNS DESTROYED. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE RATED EF1.

*** 1 INJ *** WIDESPREAD DAMAGE IN AND AROUND HUNTSVILLE FROM 900 TO 915 TIME FRAME. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE CITY. LIGHT POLE BLOWN DOWN ON NEW TRUCK. ROOF DAMAGE TO BANK IN MIDDLE OF HUNTSVILLE. STUDENT AT SAM HOUSTON STATE UNIVERSITY WAS INJURED BY A FALLING LIMB.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN HOME DEPOT PARKING LOT ON I-45 THEN CROSSED FREEWAY INTO DODGE DEALERSHIP AND THEN LIFTED BACK INTO CLOUDS NEAR MOTEL. DAMAGE TO A PORTABLE BUILDING IN THE HOME DEPOT LOT THEN ACROSS I-45 INTO DODGE DEALERSHIP DAMAGING A FEW AUTOMOBILES AND TEARING A METAL AWNING OFF AND CARRYING IT UP AND OVER POWER LINES DROPPING IT ONTO A COUPLE OF VEHICLES, SEVERELY DAMAGING THE VEHICLES. EF0 DAMAGE .36 MILES LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE.
EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT CAR DEALER SHIP LIFTING METAL AWNING IN THE AIR AND MOVING IT 100 YARDS AWAY ON TOP OF TWO CARS. MINOR DAMAGE TO THE 2 CARS.

A EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN A RESIDENTIAL SECTION OF HUNTSVILLE ON ROUNDABOUT RD. CAUSING SIGINIFICANT TREE DAMAGE. ONE OF THE TREES DID FALL ON NEARBY HOUSE CAUSING SOME ROOF DAMAGE. TREES IN THE AREA WERE BLOWN DOWN IN DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS GIVING INDICATION OF A STRONG CIRCULATION IN THE AREA.EF0 DAMAGE WITH PATH LENGTH 50 YARDS AND WIDTH 25 YARDS WIDE.

TORNADO TOUCHED ABOUT 2 MILES NORTHEAST OF SNOOK AND DID MAJOR DAMAGE TO A LARGE METAL BUILDING. THE TORNADO ALSO MOVED AND FLIPPED AN AUTOMOBILE ABOUT 50 YARDS. 5 EIGHTEEN WHEELER TRAILERS WERE TOSSED ABOUT 100 YARDS INTO A PECAN PLANTATION. A PICTURE OF THE TORNADO WAS TAKEN FROM ABOUT 3 MILES TO THE WNW. THE WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS ABOUT 75 YARDS WAS ON THE GROUND ABOUT 2 MILES. IT IS RATED AS AN EF2.

WORST OF THE DAMAGE WAS ALONG HIGHWAY 287 ONE MILE NORTH OF PENNINGTON. A FEED STORE AND RESTAURANT WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED BY AN EF-3 TORNADO WITH DEBRIS SCATTERED IN A FIELD TO THE EAST. SEVERAL HOMES AND TRAILERS WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED ALONG THE PATH. MOSTLY EF-1 AND EF-2 DAMAGE WITH NO SERIOUS INJURIES. THE START LOCATION WAS 5 MILES WSW OF PENNINGTON AND THE END LOCATION WAS 2 MILES N OF PENNINGTON. PATH LENGTH WAS 7 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 150 MPH.
